title.es: Grid Approximation, Markov Chains y MCMC Metropolis
title.en: Grid Approximation, Markov Chains and MCMC Metropolis

summary_es: Este modulo cubre los tres niveles de inferencia computacional bayesiana. Grid approximation discretiza el posterior en una grilla y funciona perfectamente para 1-2 parametros, pero sufre la maldicion de dimensionalidad con mas. Las Markov chains se introducen con estados de mercado (bear/stagnant/bull), demostrando convergencia a distribucion estacionaria. MCMC Metropolis se implementa desde cero para estimar los grados de libertad (nu) de una Student-t en retornos financieros, incluyendo diagnosticos completos: trace plots, tasa de aceptacion, autocorrelacion y convergencia. Se comparan las tres tecnicas y se conecta con HMC/NUTS (PyMC) como siguiente paso.
summary_en: This module covers three levels of Bayesian computational inference. Grid approximation discretizes the posterior on a grid, working perfectly for 1-2 parameters but suffering dimensionality curse beyond that. Markov chains are introduced with market states (bear/stagnant/bull), demonstrating convergence to stationary distribution. MCMC Metropolis is implemented from scratch to estimate Student-t degrees of freedom (nu) in financial returns, including complete diagnostics: trace plots, acceptance rate, autocorrelation, and convergence. The three techniques are compared and connected to HMC/NUTS (PyMC) as the next step.
learning_objectives_es: Implementar grid approximation y entender la maldicion de dimensionalidad
Simular Markov chains de estados de mercado con convergencia estacionaria
Implementar MCMC Metropolis desde cero para posteriors no-analiticos
Evaluar diagnosticos de MCMC: trace plots, aceptacion, autocorrelacion
Comparar grid, Metropolis y HMC como pipeline de complejidad creciente
learning_objectives_en: Implement grid approximation and understand dimensionality curse
Simulate market state Markov chains with stationary convergence
Implement MCMC Metropolis from scratch for non-analytical posteriors
Evaluate MCMC diagnostics: trace plots, acceptance, autocorrelation
Compare grid, Metropolis, and HMC as an increasing complexity pipeline

parameters.name: Computational Bayesian Inference
parameters.origin_phrase: Grid, Metropolis, and HMC form a pipeline of increasing computational power

parameters.name: Fat Tails via MCMC
parameters.origin_phrase: MCMC estimates Student-t nu directly from data without analytical formulas

parameters.name: Diagnostic Transparency
parameters.origin_phrase: Trace plots and acceptance rates make MCMC behavior inspectable
